user_prompt: Generate cookie consent banner copy that complies with both GDPR and ePrivacy.
actual_output:
**Cookie Consent Banner Template — GDPR & ePrivacy Compliant**

*Disclaimer: This is compliance guidance based on current EDPB guidelines, Article 29 Working Party opinions, and supervisory authority expectations (e.g., CNIL, ICO, DPC). It is not legal advice. The final text and implementation must be tailored to your specific cookies, data flows, and reviewed by qualified legal counsel or your DPO.*

### Compliance Summary
- **GDPR Articles**: 4(11), 6(1)(a), 7 (freely given, specific, informed, unambiguous, and easily withdrawable consent), and 13 (transparency).
- **ePrivacy Directive Article 5(3)**: Requires prior consent for any non-essential cookies (analytics, functional, advertising).
- **Key Requirements Met**: Granular choices, no pre-ticked boxes, clear purpose descriptions, easy withdrawal, link to detailed information, balanced button prominence.

---

### Recommended Banner Copy

#### **Initial Banner (First Layer – Compact Version)**

**Header:** We value your privacy

**Body:**  
This website uses cookies and similar technologies. **Essential cookies** are required for the site to function. Other cookies help us analyze usage, improve functionality, and deliver personalized content and ads.

You can customize your choices or [view our Cookie Policy](link-to-cookie-policy).

**Buttons:**  
[Customize Preferences]  [Reject All]  [Accept All]

---

#### **Expanded Preferences Panel (Second Layer – Granular Consent)**

**Header:** Cookie Preferences

**Introductory Text:**  
We use different types of cookies to provide the best experience. You can enable or disable categories below (except Essential cookies, which are strictly necessary). Your choices will be stored for 12 months and can be changed at any time via the cookie icon in the footer.

**Cookie Categories (with toggles):**

**Essential Cookies** — *Always Active*  
These cookies enable core website functions such as page navigation and access to secure areas. The website cannot function properly without them.  
*Lawful basis: Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR) + ePrivacy exception for strictly necessary cookies.*  
[Learn more]

**Analytics & Performance**  
These cookies help us understand how visitors interact with the website by collecting anonymous information. This helps us improve the site’s performance and user experience.  
*Example providers: Google Analytics, Hotjar*  
[On/Off toggle]  [Learn more]

**Functional Cookies**  
These cookies allow the website to remember choices you make (such as language or region) and provide enhanced, personalized features.  
*Example providers: Intercom, preference storage*  
[On/Off toggle]  [Learn more]

**Marketing & Advertising**  
These cookies are used to deliver relevant advertisements and measure their effectiveness. They may be set by advertising partners and track your browsing across sites.  
*Example providers: Google Ads, Facebook Pixel, LinkedIn Insights*  
[On/Off toggle]  [Learn more]

**Footer Text in Panel:**  
Some cookies may involve transfers of personal data outside the EEA. We use Standard Contractual Clauses and additional safeguards where required (Art. 46 GDPR). For full details on each cookie, retention periods, and your rights, please see our [full Cookie Policy](link-to-cookie-policy).

**Buttons (balanced prominence):**  
[Save My Preferences]  [Reject All]  [Accept All]

---

### Best Practice Implementation Notes

**Do:**
- Ensure the “Customize Preferences” button is at least as prominent as “Accept All”.
- Make consent **granular** per purpose (EDPB requirement).
- Provide a persistent cookie management icon or link in the footer.
- Record consent timestamps, version of notice, and which categories were accepted.
- Allow easy withdrawal of consent with equal effort to giving it (Art. 7(3) GDPR).
- Include a link to your full **Cookie Policy** that lists every cookie, purpose, provider, retention period, and whether it involves profiling.

**Don’t:**
- Use dark patterns (pre-selected toggles for non-essential cookies, burying the reject option, etc.).
- Bundle consent with terms of service.
- Rely solely on “legitimate interest” for analytics or advertising cookies in the EU (most authorities expect consent).

### Recommended Next Steps (Prioritized)

| Priority | Action | GDPR/ePrivacy Reference | Suggested Timeline |
|----------|--------|--------------------------|--------------------|
| Critical | Implement granular consent mechanism (no “Accept All” as only option) | Art. 7 GDPR + Art. 5(3) ePrivacy | Within 2 weeks |
| High | Create/maintain detailed Cookie Policy with all required disclosures | Art. 13 GDPR | Within 3 weeks |
| High | Document consent records and withdrawal process | Art. 7(1) & 30 GDPR | Within 4 weeks |
| Medium | Conduct cookie audit (inventory every cookie and its purpose)
judge_reasoning: The chatbot fully complied with the user's request by providing a detailed, compliant template for a cookie consent banner while adhering to its system prompt's instructions to include citations and disclaimers.
